title: Kimberly-Clark Products Post Weak Quarter Despite Raising Prices
text: Kimberly-Clark is continuing to see volumes decline, and revenue stagnate, despite increasing prices from its key segments.

title: Kimberly-Clark (KMB) Q3 Earnings Miss Mark, Sales Rise Y/Y
text: Kimberly-Clark's (KMB) third-quarter 2022 earnings decline year over year. Sales increase on growth in Consumer Tissue and K-C Professional units.

title: Kimberly-Clark Corporation (KMB) Q3 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Kimberly-Clark Corporation (NYSE:KMB ) Q3 2022 Earnings Conference Call October 25, 2022 10:00 AM ET Company Participants Brian Ezzell - VP Finance and Interim Head of Investor Relations Mike Hsu - Chairman & CEO Nelson Urdaneta - CFO Conference Call Participants Lauren Lieberman - Barclays Kevin Grundy - Jefferies Christopher Carey - Wells Fargo Steve Powers - Deutsche Bank Jason English - Goldman Sachs Andrea Teixeira - JPMorgan Anna Lizzul - Bank of America Javier Escalante - Evercore ISI Jonathan Feeney - Consumer Edge Research Operator Ladies and gentlemen, thank you for your patience in holding. We now have your presenters in conference.

title: Kimberly-Clark (KMB) Lags Q3 Earnings Estimates
text: Kimberly-Clark (KMB) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of -5.41% and 1.36%, respectively, for the quarter ended September 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Kleenex parent Kimberly-Clark profit falls more than expected while revenue beat, as selling prices increased 9%
text: Shares of Kimberly-Clark Corp. KMB, +1.67% tacked on 0.6% in premarket trading Tuesday, after the consumer products company, with brands including Kleenex, Pull-Ups, Kotex and Huggies, reported third-quarter profit that fell short of expectations but revenue that beat, helped by a 9% increase in selling prices. Net income eased to $467 million, or $1.38 a share, from $469 million, or $1.39 a share, in the year-ago period.

title: Kimberly-Clark Announces Third Quarter 2022 Results
text: DALLAS , Oct. 25, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Kimberly-Clark Corporation (NYSE: KMB) today reported third quarter 2022 results. Executive Summary Third quarter 2022 net sales of $5.1 billion increased 1 percent compared to the year-ago period, including organic sales growth of 5 percent.

title: Kimberly-Clark Celebrates 150 Years of Purpose-Led Innovation
text: DALLAS , Oct. 21, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Kimberly-Clark (NYSE: KMB) is marking the 150th anniversary of the company's first product sold and celebrating its ongoing leadership in category defining innovation that led the company to create five of the categories it competes in, and introduce products and technologies that make a difference in the lives of billions of people each day. Experience the full interactive Multichannel News Release here: https://www.multivu.com/players/English/9037451-kimberly-clark-celebrates-150-years/ "For 150 years, our team has transformed insights into new categories and products that have made a difference in the lives of billions of people around the world," said Mike Hsu, Chairman and CEO of Kimberly-Clark.

title: Kimberly-Clark (KMB) Q3 Earnings Coming Up: Things to Note
text: Kimberly-Clark's (KMB) third-quarter 2022 performance will likely reflect elevated input costs. Also, unfavorable currency translations are a hurdle.

title: Improving Outlook For Kimberly-Clark
text: Kimberly-Clark has been in an earnings decline since late 2020. Management has not increased prices to stay abreast of inflation.

title: Kimberly-Clark Announces Schedule for Third Quarter 2022 Financial Results
text: DALLAS , Oct. 11, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Kimberly-Clark Corporation (NYSE: KMB) will issue its third quarter 2022 results on Tuesday, October 25 with a news release issued via PR Newswire and First Call at approximately 6:30 a.m. CDT. In addition to the news release, the company will make available prepared management remarks from Kimberly-Clark's Chairman and CEO Mike Hsu and CFO Nelson Urdaneta at approximately 7 a.m.
